location.office_time_required: three days per week in office - Monday, Wednesday and Thursday (starting February 2, 2026); up to four weeks per year of fully remote work

secondary_archetype: Director of SEO / Organic Growth & Discoverability

level_signals: 8+ years in SEO, organic growth, or digital marketing
at least 3 years leading a team and owning strategy and budget
Reports to Head of Growth Marketing
Own the roadmap, OKRs, and budget

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years in SEO, organic growth, or digital marketing, with at least 3 years leading a team and owning strategy and budget at a B2B SaaS, fintech, or technology company.
at least 3 years leading a team and owning strategy and budget at a B2B SaaS, fintech, or technology company.
You connect organic channels to pipeline, revenue, and business outcomes, not just traffic and rankings. You think in SQLs, activation rates, and CAC efficiency.
You can read crawl logs, diagnose Core Web Vitals issues, direct schema implementations, and partner credibly with engineers on site architecture decisions.
You've actively experimented with citation strategies, structured content for model consumption, or measured share of voice in answer engines. We can't train this on the job.
You can tell the difference between content that earns trust and content that fills a page. You have experience building or managing content operations at scale with high editorial standards.
You've managed agencies and know how to hold them to outcomes, not just deliverables. You also know when to stop outsourcing and bring work in-house.
You make consequential decisions with incomplete data. You like building a function from scratch more than running one that's already humming.
You treat failure as a feedback loop. You run experiments, kill what doesn't work quickly, and double down on what does.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've navigated a major Google algorithm update that materially impacted your business and can walk through how you diagnosed, adapted, and recovered.
You understand how retrieval-augmented generation systems select and rank sources, and you can translate that into actionable content and structural strategy.
You use AI and automation tools in your own workflows. You're a practitioner, not just a strategist.
Experience in a regulated or compliance-sensitive industry.

scope.team_size_estimate: unclear (inherits existing team, grows it from there)
scope.surface: Organic growth and discoverability across Google, AI answer engines, Reddit, G2, YouTube — from first discovery to qualified lead
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — corporate cards, expense management, business banking
primary_metrics_inferred: Conversion from organic visitors to activated users and pipeline
Share of voice in AI-generated answers
core_responsibilities: Set the organic growth strategy and own the roadmap, OKRs, and budget
Build Brex's AEO/GEO playbook for ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude, and Google AI Overviews
Co-own the technical SEO roadmap with web engineering
Run content strategy and production for organic
Unify presence across Reddit, G2, YouTube, and other community and review surfaces
Get sales and partnerships up to speed on how organic and AI search are changing
Be a culture carrier for the Growth Marketing team

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($220K-$276K base)
Clear ownership scope — no single person owns visibility today, giving greenfield authority
Explicit 12-18 month success criteria stated in JD
AI-native working culture described with specifics (Claude, Clay, custom integrations)
Reports directly to Head of Growth Marketing — senior access
Team carries a number (SQL ARR, bookings, activations) — accountability is clear
Equity mentioned as part of total comp
red_flags: Role is Seattle office-based hybrid; moving to 3 days/week in-office from February 2026 — no remote option
AI visibility fluency is explicitly non-trainable: 'We can't train this on the job' — hard bar with no mitigation path
Brex is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Capital One — may affect startup culture/compensation dynamics for candidates seeking pure startup environment
Inheriting an existing team of unclear size and composition adds onboarding complexity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, team leadership requirement, and comp range)
scope.team_size_estimate (not stated; described as 'inherit some of it, grow it from there')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and Growth Marketing section; not fully enumerated in Requirements)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on... the candidate's location')
ambiguities: JD states 'up to four weeks per year of fully remote work' as a perk but also states the role is based in Seattle office — remote_us_eligible set to false as the role requires Seattle office presence; the 4-week remote perk is supplemental, not full remote eligibility.
The office schedule describes a transition: currently 2 days/week (Wed/Thu), increasing to 3 days/week (Mon/Wed/Thu) from February 2, 2026. It is unclear which applies at time of hire.
